scene_summary: A corporate meeting where executives blame a shortfall in March performance on Charles's low golf outing cadence and argue that increasing his golf trips will free up time for team forums.

signature_lines: where we dive too deep into our April roadmap
observations.interpersonal_note: sets the tone for the meeting by immediately blaming Charles's golf cadence for March's shortfall

signature_lines: are a massively critical input for us
observations.interpersonal_note: defends Charles's golf outings as essential to pipeline generation while also agreeing they need more team time

signature_lines: And from a pipeline generation standpoint, Charles' odd course partner engagements are a massively critical input for us.
observations.interpersonal_note: echoes the boss's framing and adds that more golf trips would enable scheduled forums
pure agreement with the boss's framing without adding new content

signature_lines: And from a meeting perspective, right,
observations.interpersonal_note: introduces the 'meeting' angle as another reason to increase Charles's golf cadence

signature_lines: hopefully you're still able to regularly elevate our discourse, right?
observations.interpersonal_note: closes the loop by agreeing that golf trips will enable more forums and thus better discourse
